events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are asked to assess compliance: PVC expansion fitting omitted on long exterior run. The work is rough-complete. Reference: NEC Art. 344 (Rigid Metal Conduit) and 352 (Rigid Polyvinyl Chloride Conduit). The scenario is PVC conduit, so NEC 352 applies. Specifically, 352.44 requires expansion fittings for PVC conduit to compensate for thermal expansion and contraction. For exterior runs, temperature changes are significant. Omission of an expansion fitting on a long exterior run is a code violation. So decision: fail. Risk: medium? Could be high if conduit pulls apart or damages conductors. But it's rough-complete, so not yet closed out, so it's a defect that needs correction. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete: maybe 90%? Rough-complete means installed but not closed out, so maybe 90%? I'll estimate 90. Value: 0 (no numeric reading). Sound source: not applicable. Confidence: high, 0.95. Event time, rate, order, workable: not needed. Findings: "PVC expansion fitting omitted on long exterior run, violating NEC 352.44." Actions: "Install expansion fitting(s) as required by code and manufacturer instructions." Rationale: "PVC conduit expands and contracts with temperature changes; omission can cause conduit separation or damage." References: ["NEC 352.44"]. So JSON output.
